rulings.Case Title: TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY HUANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, November 10, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF TIAN SHAN TRADING (USA), INCORPORATED'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint IS Denied. Moving party has not shown how it is in the furtherance of justice to permit plaintiff to amend pleading to add new causes of action against an individual who has already been dismissed with prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JANET GONG VS. MIRIAM PAVLOVA NALLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-11-838382 WITH CASE CGC-11-515186
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 10, 2011, Line 21, PLAINTIFF JANET GONG MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-11-838382 WITH CASE CGC-11-515186. Denied. Trial date has already been set and continued twice in the small claims case. The Court finds that consolidation is prejudicial to Defendants and will not avoid unnecessary costs or delay. See CCP § 1048(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
